questions.question: Who was the first to observe Saturn with a telescope?
questions.explanation: Saturn has been known to us because it is easily visible to the naked eye. Galileo Galilei was the first to observe Saturn with a telescope in 1610.

questions.question: Which among the following is formed by the process of Lithification?
questions.explanation: Lithification gives rise to sedimentary rocks. Out of the above, only Feldspar is a sedimentary rock.

questions.question: Which among the following is a Treeless Biome?
questions.explanation: Tundra is the coldest of all the biomes. Tundra comes from the Finnish word tunturi, meaning treeless plain. It is present in the frost-molded landscapes, extremely low temperatures, little precipitation, poor nutrients, and short growing seasons. It is a treeless biome.

questions.question: Which country has the largest reserves of Natural Gas in the world?
questions.explanation: Russia has the largest natural gas reserves in the world, with 38 trillion cubic meters (1,341 trillion cubic feet). This is equivalent to about 19% of the world’s total reserves. Russia exports 26% of its gas production to other countries. The Arctic accounts for over 80% of Russia’s natural gas production. The top five countries with the largest natural gas reserves are: Russia Iran Qatar USA Saudi Arabia The world has proven reserves equivalent to 52.3 times its annual consumption. This means it has about 52 years of gas left at current consumption levels.

questions.question: With respect to Geomorphology, what are ‘Inselbergs’?

options.label: A
options.text: Desert counterpart of the delta formed due to depositional aeolian action

options.label: B
options.text: Residual hills formed due to erosional aeolian action

options.label: C
options.text: Semi-circular steepsided depression formed through depositional glacial action

options.label: D
options.text: Landform created due to combined action of fluvial erosional and depositional

answer.label: B
answer.text: Residual hills formed due to erosional aeolian action

questions.explanation: Inselberg are similar to the monadnocks found in peneplains.They are formed due to erosional aeolian action.

questions.question: Which of the following evidences convey the conclusion that Asia and Africa had a single landmass? (UPSC Prelims 1982)

options.label: A
options.text: The deserts in Asia and Africa

options.label: B
options.text: The similarity in climate of certain regions of Asia and Africa

options.label: C
options.text: The finding of same type of fossil in both the regions

questions.explanation: The similarity in the deserts in Asia and Africa, the similarity in climate of certain regions of Asia and Africa, the same type of fossil in both the regions are the clear evidences that Asia and Africa had a single landmass.

questions.question: Which of the following archipelago comprises the Commonwealth of the Bahamas and the British Territory of the Turks and Caicos Islands?
questions.explanation: The Lucayan Archipelago is also called the Bahama Archipelago in the North Atlantic Ocean. It is an island group comprising the Commonwealth of the Bahamas and the British overseas territory of the Turks and Caicos islands.

questions.question: Which is the world’s largest island country?
questions.explanation: Indonesia is the world’s largest island country as it has an area of 1,904,569 square km and it comprises more than 18,307 islands. It is also the most populous island country in the world.

questions.question: Which among the following is not a gulf in the Pacific Ocean?
questions.explanation: Gulf of Mannar is not a part of the Pacific Ocean. It is a large shallow bay forming the part of the Laccadive Sea in the Indian Ocean. It lies between Sri Lanka and the Coromandel Coast region of India.

questions.question: Which of the following canyon is not found in the United States?
questions.explanation: Windy Canyon is located in New Zealand. It is found in the centre east of the Great Barrier Island in New Zealand. Its shape is like a wind funnel.
